title: Connelly Early once again gave losing Red Sox a chance to win, but failed to pitch deeper into the game
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Connelly Early allowed one run through 4.1 innings in the Red Sox's 3-2 loss to the Cardinals.
Early struggled with command, resulting in high pitch counts and early exit without a personal win this season.
Red Sox's road record fell to 1-6, with their starter ERA at 4.81 after Kelly relinquished the lead in relief.

title: It’s been decades since a center won the NBA Most Improved Player award. Will Neemias Queta be next?
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Neemias Queta of the Celtics significantly improved, averaging double minutes and achieving 17 double-doubles this season.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla emphasizes Queta's growth and defensive role, suggesting him as a Most Improved Player contender.
Queta is a leading candidate for NBA Most Improved Player; a center hasn't won since Jermaine O'Neal in 2002.

title: The Red Sox need to shake up the top of their lineup to avoid slow starts
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The Red Sox's top four batters only produced one hit against pitcher Dustin May in a recent game.
Manager Alex Cora is considering lineup changes, such as promoting Masataka Yoshida or Ceddanne Rafaela to leadoff.
Potential call-ups like Mickey Gasper from Worcester are being contemplated to improve offensive production.

title: Celtics clinch No. 2 seed in Eastern Conference playoffs with blowout win over Pelicans
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Celtics secured the No. 2 seed in the Eastern Conference playoffs by defeating the New Orleans Pelicans 144-118.
The Celtics tied the NBA single-game three-point record with 29 threes in their penultimate regular season game.
Boston's playoff opponent will be either the Orlando Magic or the Philadelphia 76ers, determined by the play-in tournament.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Pizza Movie' and 'Sirat' are new films streaming on Hulu, featuring unique comedic and dramatic storylines, respectively.
'The Boys' Season 5 continues its dark, politically charged narrative, now available on Prime Video.
'Hacks' and 'The Testaments' Season 5 are streaming on HBO Max and Hulu, continuing popular TV series journeys.

title: Jayson Tatum won’t play in Celtics’ penultimate regular season matchup
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum will miss the Celtics' game against the Pelicans due to right Achilles repair management.
Jaylen Brown is dealing with left Achilles tendinitis but will be available to play against New Orleans.
Boston's starters Sam Hauser, Neemias Queta, and Derrick White are listed as probable but expected to play.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her daughter’s courage through cancer treatment
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lori Robertson, from Concord, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her daughter Emma.
Emma Robertson, aged 16, was diagnosed with medulloblastoma, a brain tumor, in April 2025.
Lori's run also supports the medical teams at Massachusetts General Hospital aiding Emma's treatment.

title: Bruins fans will need to wait a little longer for James Hagens’s NHL debut
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens, Boston Bruins top prospect, won't debut against Tampa Bay Lightning on Saturday.
Coach Marco Sturm emphasizes patience, with no guarantee Hagens will play in remaining three regular-season games.
Bruins aim to secure playoff spot with a win against Tampa Bay after missing postseason last year.

title: Boston billionaires, historic figures land on Forbes’ ‘self-made’ lists
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Forbes ranks Noubar Afeyan, Bill Cummings, and Herb Chambers among the greatest self-made Americans from Boston.
Historic figures with Boston ties on Forbes' list include Benjamin Franklin, Babe Ruth, and Louisa May Alcott.
Forbes' rankings consider financial success, obstacles overcome, and long-term impact on self-made individuals.

title: What you need to know about the 2026 Boston Marathon
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The 130th Boston Marathon will take place on April 20, 2026, starting in Hopkinton and ending at Copley Square.
This year's marathon features 30,000 participants from over 130 countries and all 50 U.S. states, split into six start waves.
Live coverage of the marathon begins at 4 a.m. on WCVB Channel 5, with streaming available on the Very Local app.
